body:
### Problem

The highlighted sections in `:EditQuery` don't match the results from `tree-sitter-cli`. They only highlight the `@i` captures and ignore all the other captures. (See the steps to reproduce below)

This is not a highlighting issue because `vim.treesitter.query.parse(...):iter_matches(...)` does not return the correct values either.

Relevant print logs using `vim.print({id, name, i, node:type(), vim.treesitter.get_node_text(node, bufnr), r1, c1, r2, c2})` where `r1, c1, r2, c2` are from `node:range()`:
```
get selection for (decorated_definition . (decorator)+ @decorator):
{ 1, "decorator", 1, "decorator", '@pytest.mark.skipif(False, "Some long explanation here")', 0, 0, 0, 56 }
{ 1, "decorator", 2, "decorator", "@decorator", 1, 0, 1, 10 }
{ 1, "decorator", 3, "decorator", "@override", 2, 0, 2, 9 }
{ 1, "decorator", 4, "decorator", "@x:=3", 3, 0, 3, 5 }
{ 1, "decorator", 5, "decorator", "@(foo())", 4, 0, 4, 8 }

get selection for (decorator . (
    "@" @at
    .
    [
        [(_ !function) (ERROR)]+ @i
        (parenthesized_expression . "(" @pp (_)? @pinner ")" @pp .)
        (call function: [(_) (ERROR)] @f arguments: (argument_list . "(" @cp ([(_) (ERROR)] ","?)* @cinner ")" @cp .))
    ]
  ) (#not-match? @i "^\\(")
):
{ 1, "at", 1, "@", "@", 1, 0, 1, 1 }
{ 2, "i", 1, "identifier", "decorator", 1, 1, 1, 10 }
{ 1, "at", 1, "@", "@", 2, 0, 2, 1 }
{ 2, "i", 1, "identifier", "override", 2, 1, 2, 9 }
{ 1, "at", 1, "@", "@", 3, 0, 3, 1 }
{ 2, "i", 1, "named_expression", "x:=3", 3, 1, 3, 5 }
```

Possibly related: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/20948 , but even without predicates it still is inconsistent. Removing the `#not-match?` will capture all the correct subtrees but with some duplicate captures not present in tree-sitter-cli.

### Steps to reproduce

~~Download the latest `python.so` using the `nvim-treesitter` plugin~~
Download and build the python tree-sitter repo
```bash
git clone https://github.com/tree-sitter/tree-sitter-python
cd tree-sitter-python
git checkout v0.25.0
tree-sitter build -o python.so
cp python.so /home/user/.local/share/nvim/site/parser/python.so
```
 and add the following line in `contrib/minimal.lua`:
```lua
vim.treesitter.language.add('python', { path = "/home/user/.local/share/nvim/site/parser/python.so" })
```

Create the file `file.py`:
```python
@pytest.mark.skipif(False, "Some long explanation here")
@decorator
@override
@x:=3
@(foo())
class Foo(object):
    ...
```

Create the file `test.scm`:
```scm
(decorator . (
    "@" @at
    .
    [
        [(_ !function) (ERROR)]+ @i
        (parenthesized_expression . "(" @pp (_)? @pinner ")" @pp .)
        (call function: [(_) (ERROR)] @f arguments: (argument_list . "(" @cp ([(_) (ERROR)] ","?)* @cinner ")" @cp .))
    ]
  ) (#not-match? @i "^\\(")
)
```

Start neovim:
```bash
nvim --clean -u contrib/minimal.lua file.py
```

Running `:InspectTree` will show:
```scm
(module ; [0, 0] - [7, 0]
  (decorated_definition ; [0, 0] - [6, 7]
    (decorator ; [0, 0] - [0, 56]
      (call ; [0, 1] - [0, 56]
        function: (attribute ; [0, 1] - [0, 19]
          object: (attribute ; [0, 1] - [0, 12]
            object: (identifier) ; [0, 1] - [0, 7]
            attribute: (identifier)) ; [0, 8] - [0, 12]
          attribute: (identifier)) ; [0, 13] - [0, 19]
        arguments: (argument_list ; [0, 19] - [0, 56]
          (false) ; [0, 20] - [0, 25]
          (string ; [0, 27] - [0, 55]
            (string_start) ; [0, 27] - [0, 28]
            (string_content) ; [0, 28] - [0, 54]
            (string_end))))) ; [0, 54] - [0, 55]
    (decorator ; [1, 0] - [1, 10]
      (identifier)) ; [1, 1] - [1, 10]
    (decorator ; [2, 0] - [2, 9]
      (identifier)) ; [2, 1] - [2, 9]
    (decorator ; [3, 0] - [3, 5]
      (named_expression ; [3, 1] - [3, 5]
        name: (identifier) ; [3, 1] - [3, 2]
        value: (integer))) ; [3, 4] - [3, 5]
    (decorator ; [4, 0] - [4, 8]
      (parenthesized_expression ; [4, 1] - [4, 8]
        (call ; [4, 2] - [4, 7]
          function: (identifier) ; [4, 2] - [4, 5]
          arguments: (argument_list)))) ; [4, 5] - [4, 7]
    definition: (class_definition ; [5, 0] - [6, 7]
      name: (identifier) ; [5, 6] - [5, 9]
      superclasses: (argument_list ; [5, 9] - [5, 17]
        (identifier)) ; [5, 10] - [5, 16]
      body: (block ; [6, 4] - [6, 7]
        (expression_statement ; [6, 4] - [6, 7]
          (ellipsis)))))) ; [6, 4] - [6, 7]
```

Run `:EditQuery` with the contents of `test.scm` and hover cursor over the various `@` captures.

### Expected behavior

Run:
```bash
$ tree-sitter query --lib-path /home/user/.local/share/nvim/site/parser/python.so test.scm file.py
file.py
  pattern: 0
    capture: 0 - at, start: (0, 0), end: (0, 1), text: `@`
    capture: 4 - f, start: (0, 1), end: (0, 19), text: `pytest.mark.skipif`
    capture: 5 - cp, start: (0, 19), end: (0, 20), text: `(`
    capture: 6 - cinner, start: (0, 20), end: (0, 25), text: `False`
    capture: 6 - cinner, start: (0, 27), end: (0, 55), text: `"Some long explanation here"`
    capture: 5 - cp, start: (0, 55), end: (0, 56), text: `)`
  pattern: 0
    capture: 0 - at, start: (1, 0), end: (1, 1), text: `@`
    capture: 1 - i, start: (1, 1), end: (1, 10), text: `decorator`
  pattern: 0
    capture: 0 - at, start: (2, 0), end: (2, 1), text: `@`
    capture: 1 - i, start: (2, 1), end: (2, 9), text: `override`
  pattern: 0
    capture: 0 - at, start: (3, 0), end: (3, 1), text: `@`
    capture: 1 - i, start: (3, 1), end: (3, 5), text: `x:=3`
  pattern: 0
    capture: 0 - at, start: (4, 0), end: (4, 1), text: `@`
    capture: 2 - pp, start: (4, 1), end: (4, 2), text: `(`
    capture: 3 - pinner, start: (4, 2), end: (4, 7), text: `foo()`
    capture: 2 - pp, start: (4, 7), end: (4, 8), text: `)`
```
with the latest tree-sitter version (current 0.26.7) but you can also reproduce with earlier versions. This is the same version as bundled with neovim.

Notice how it captures all the sections of the decorators with nothing missing and no duplicates.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

NVIM v0.12.0-dev-2724+g64d55b74d8-dirty Build type: RelWithDebInfo LuaJIT 2.1.1774638290 Run "nvim -V1 -v" for more info

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

Normal vim doesn't use tree-sitter.

### Operating system/version

Debian GNU/Linux 13 (trixie)

### Terminal name/version

# GNOME Terminal 3.56.2 using VTE 0.80.1 +BIDI +GNUTLS +ICU +SYSTEMD

### $TERM environment variable

xterm-256color

### Installation

Built on 2026-03-28 from repo on branch HEAD -> master -> 64d55b74d83d566975e269bed0810d9008119ddf (but reproducible much earlier)
title: Inconsistent and broken behaviour between neovim and tree-sitter-cli
